name: Danube — Budapest (Pest bank)
notes: Main stem Danube at Budapest. Spring snowmelt from Alps still elevated. Monitoring ongoing by OVIT.

name: Soroksári-Duna (Southern Danube arm)
notes: Regulated side-arm of the Danube. Sluice gates control flow. Key irrigation source for southern Pest farms.

name: Ráckeve–Soroksár Canal
notes: Managed irrigation and navigation canal. Currently 37% below normal level — drought risk for Ráckeve district.

name: Velencei-tó (Lake Velence)
notes: Shallow lake, critically low this April. Evaporation and reduced inflow. Fishing and recreation bans in effect.

name: Homokhátság Irrigation Canal
notes: Primary irrigation canal for the sandy Homokhátság region. Critically low — drought Level II advisory active.

name: Zagyva River
notes: Zagyva tributary of the Tisza. Spring flow slightly above normal from upstream rainfall. Stable.

name: Galga Stream (Aszód)
notes: Small stream in the northern Pest hills. Below normal due to dry April. Limited irrigation potential.

name: Gödöllő Retention Reservoir
notes: Municipal reservoir. Currently at 84% capacity. Serves urban supply and emergency irrigation buffer.

name: Gerje–Perje Canal (Cegléd)
notes: Historic irrigation canal serving the Cegléd–Nagykőrös vegetable belt. Flow 20% below seasonal average.

name: Tápió River (Tápiószentmárton)
notes: Tápió catchment river. Received recent rainfall — currently near normal levels. Usable for supplemental irrigation.

name: Bugyi Oxbow Lake
notes: Former Danube oxbow. Stagnant, low inflow. Slight drought risk but good wildlife habitat. Not suitable for irrigation.

name: Duna–Tisza Canal (Ráckeve section)
notes: Inter-basin transfer canal. Severely reduced flow — this section shows the strongest drought signal in the county.
